steps.title: Read a point from the original line of best fit
steps.content: From the scatterplot for data set P, the line of best fit passes through approximately $(100, 14)$. This point will be used to test each candidate equation.

steps.title: Apply the horizontal shift
steps.content: Data set Q is created by adding $540$ to every $x$-value of P. A horizontal shift does not change slope; it slides each point right by $540$. So $(100,14)$ becomes $(640,14)$. Graph the four candidate equations and plot $(640,14)$; the correct model should pass near that shifted point with the original slope.

steps.title: Test each choice at x = 640
steps.content:
• A. $y = -3 + \frac{35}{1{,}301}x$ at $x=640$: $y = -3 + \frac{35 \cdot 640}{1301} = -3 + \frac{22400}{1301} \approx -3 + 17.22 \approx 14.2$. Matches $(640, 14)$.
  • B. $y = 9 + \frac{1}{131}x$ at $x=640$: $y = 9 + \frac{640}{131} \approx 9 + 4.89 \approx 13.9$. Close to 14, but the slope $\frac{1}{131} \approx 0.0076$ is much smaller than P's visible slope, so the original P line would not match. Eliminated.
  • C. $y = 18 + \frac{1}{131}x$ at $x=640$: $y \approx 18 + 4.89 \approx 22.9$. Far from 14. Eliminated.
  • D. $y = 26 + \frac{35}{1{,}301}x$ at $x=640$: $y \approx 26 + 17.22 \approx 43.2$. Far from 14. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
